name: Tool Integration
description: Connect AI agents to existing systems through configurable tools

content.title: Tool Integration
content.content:
# Tool Integration

AI agents need to interact with the real world - read documents, query databases, update systems. MeshWeaver provides a configurable tool layer that connects agents to existing infrastructure.

## What Are Tools?

Tools are capabilities that AI agents can invoke:

```mermaid
graph TD
    subgraph "AI Agent"
        A[Agent Logic]
    end

    subgraph "Available Tools"
        B[Read Document]
        C[Query Data Mesh]
        D[Update Record]
        E[Send Notification]
    end

    subgraph "External Systems"
        F[Document Store]
        G[Databases]
        H[Workflow System]
        I[Email]
    end

    A -->|invoke| B --> F
    A -->|invoke| C --> G
    A -->|invoke| D --> H
    A -->|invoke| E --> I
```

## Built-in Tools

| Tool Category | Capabilities |
|---------------|-------------|
| **Data Mesh Query** | Access any addressable data - records, documents, metadata |
| **Document Parser** | Extract content from PDFs, Excel, Word |
| **Vector Search** | Semantic search across indexed content |
| **Business Rules** | Apply configured validation rules |
| **Workflow Trigger** | Initiate downstream processes |

## External Tool Connectors

| System Type | Integration |
|-------------|-------------|
| **Document Management** | REST API connector |
| **Email (Outlook)** | Microsoft Graph API |
| **Cloud Storage** | Native connectors |
| **Databases** | SQL/NoSQL connectors |
| **Custom APIs** | Configurable REST endpoints |

## Tool Configuration

Tools are defined as data in the mesh - configurable without code changes:

```yaml
tool:
  name: queryContracts
  description: Search and retrieve contract information
  endpoint: /api/contracts/search
  parameters:
    - name: clientId
      type: string
      required: false
    - name: dateRange
      type: dateRange
      required: false
  returns:
    type: contractList
```

## Agent-Tool Mapping

Different agents have access to different tools:

```mermaid
graph LR
    subgraph "Research Agent"
        R[Can search, can read]
    end
    
    subgraph "Processing Agent"
        P[Can read, can write]
    end
    
    subgraph "Notification Agent"
        N[Can read, can notify]
    end
    
    subgraph "Tools"
        T1[Search]
        T2[Read]
        T3[Write]
        T4[Notify]
    end
    
    R --> T1
    R --> T2
    P --> T2
    P --> T3
    N --> T2
    N --> T4
```

## Security & Governance

| Concern | How It's Addressed |
|---------|-------------------|
| **Access Control** | Agents only access permitted tools |
| **Audit Trail** | Every tool invocation logged |
| **Rate Limiting** | Configurable limits per tool |
| **Validation** | Input/output schema enforcement |

## Benefits

- **Extensibility**: Add new tools without code changes
- **Security**: Fine-grained tool permissions
- **Reusability**: Tools shared across agents
- **Observability**: Full trace of tool usage
- **Flexibility**: Connect to any system with REST API
